exploration_log.aha_moment: OnRetryArguments already has a `Duration` property and is constructed with `executionTime` from `_timeProvider.GetElapsedTime(startTimestamp)`. The same value just needs to be passed to RetryDelayGeneratorArguments.
exploration_log.hindsight: No dead ends; the fix was straightforward once I saw `executionTime` is already computed before the `DelayGenerator` call.

alternatives: Measure duration differently in the DelayGenerator path
Add a separate timer for the delay generator

solve_notes: Added `Duration` property and constructor parameter to `RetryDelayGeneratorArguments<TResult>`, matching the pattern from `OnRetryArguments`. Updated `RetryResilienceStrategy.ExecuteCore` to pass the already-computed `executionTime` value when constructing `RetryDelayGeneratorArguments`.
task_text:
Fix RetryDelayGeneratorArguments not including previous attempt execution duration

`RetryDelayGeneratorArguments` provides the attempt number and outcome
to custom delay generators but does not include the execution duration
of the previous attempt. Delay generators that want to implement
latency-adaptive backoff (e.g., increasing delay when the upstream
is slow) must track timing externally. Fix
`RetryDelayGeneratorArguments.cs` to add a `Duration` property and
populate it from the execution time measured in
`RetryResilienceStrategy.cs`, matching the `Duration` already
available on `OnRetryArguments`.
